Throughout history, two dominant theories the origin life Earth have prevailed. Abiogenesis, theory that now widely discredited, holds that life was and spontaneously generated from decaying organic matter. The theory biogenesis, the other hand, maintains that new life produced from existing life forms.
The oldest living things Earth are believed single-cell prokaryotes, more commonly known bacteria. Scientists have discovered fossils such prokaryotes from roughly 3.5 billion years ago.
Plant life began the Earth’s landforms about 430 millions years ago. The first plants were likely single-celled organisms distantly related algae.
The first reptiles began life 300 million years ago, and modern mammals appeared roughly millions years ago.
Apes originated Earth about million years ago, and the first apelike men appeared about million years ago. The modern human species Homo sapien has existed the earth for only 100,000 years.
Scientists believe that Earth will exist for another 7.5 billion years before the sun becomes red giant star and, all probability, destroys the planet. Earth may cease support life forms far before that date, however, due increased energy output from the sun over time.
Living organisms are divided into two basic domains: prokaryotes and eukaryotes. Eukaryotes, which humans belong, have cellular structure with nucleus and genetic information, while the cells prokaryotes not contain nucleus.
